---
title: "fibroblast_signatures_aois"
output: html_document
date: "2026-05-19"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}

library(Seurat)
library(UCell)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr) # Optional: for adding p-values to plots
library(dplyr)

```


```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
# 1. Read files
fibr_gene_expression_modified <- read.csv("/Users/nikamikhailava/Desktop/Lab_stuff/batch_merged_new/fibroblasts_gene_expression_data_new_batch_three_merged_transposed.csv", check.names = FALSE)
cell_proportions <- read.csv("/Users/nikamikhailava/Desktop/Lab_stuff/batch_merged_new/bp_res_mid_lvl_ct_updated_ct_fraction_batch_3.csv", check.names = FALSE)

fibr_gene_expression_modified_genes<-fibr_gene_expression_modified

# 2. Merge using dplyr (very similar to pd.merge)
fibr_gene_expression_modified <- inner_join(
  fibr_gene_expression_modified, 
  cell_proportions, 
  by = "dcc_filename"
)
# Note: You can easily swap inner_join() with left_join(), right_join(), or full_join()

# 3. Read signature genes
signature_genes <- read.csv("/Users/nikamikhailava/Desktop/Fibroblasts_signature_derivation/stroma_vs_tsi_top100_variable_genes_pre_post.csv", sep = ";", row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE)
```



```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
fibr_gene_expression_modified
```


```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
signature_genes

```
```{r}
# fibroblast_signatures <- list(
#  TSI_Signature = c(
#     "FN1", "COL1A1", "IFI6", "COL3A1", "ISG15", 
#     "POSTN", "MMP11", "THBS1", "TIMP3", "COL11A1", 
#     "MZT2B", "DUSP1", "RSAD2", "SFRP4", "APOE"
#   )
# )

fibroblast_signatures <- list(
 TSI_Signatures = c(
    "FN1", "COL1A1", "IFI6", "COL3A1", "ISG15", 
    "POSTN", "MMP11", "THBS1", "TIMP3", "COL11A1" 
  )
)

# 1. Move the dcc_filenames column to the row names
rownames(fibr_gene_expression_modified_genes) <- fibr_gene_expression_modified_genes[, 1]

# 2. Remove that first column so the data frame is 100% numeric
fibr_gene_expression_modified_genes <- fibr_gene_expression_modified_genes[, -1]

# 3. Transpose the data frame so GENES are rows and DCC_FILENAMES are columns
expr_matrix_transposed <- t(fibr_gene_expression_modified_genes)

# 4. Now run the UCell scoring on the transposed numeric matrix
ucell_scores <- ScoreSignatures_UCell(
  matrix = expr_matrix_transposed, 
  features = fibroblast_signatures
)

ucell_df <- as.data.frame(ucell_scores)
ucell_df$dcc_filename<-rownames(ucell_df)
scores_per_aoi<-inner_join(
  ucell_df, 
  cell_proportions, 
  by = "dcc_filename"
)
# 5. Filter Data: Keep only Stroma segments
stroma_data <- scores_per_aoi %>%
  filter(Segment == "stroma")
write.csv(stroma_data, "ucell_tsi_scores_per_aoi_pre_post.csv", row.names = FALSE)
# ==========================================
# 6. VIOLIN / BOXPLOT
# ==========================================
# Plotting based ONLY on stroma_data, grouping/coloring by segment_geomx
p_violin <- ggplot(stroma_data, aes(x = Segment_geomx, y = TSI_Signatures_UCell, fill = Segment_geomx)) +
  geom_violin(trim = FALSE, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.2, fill = "white", color = "black", outlier.shape = NA) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(
    title = "TSI Signature Activity in Stroma",
    x = "GeoMx Segment",
    y = "UCell Score"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) + # Angles text if segment names are long
  stat_compare_means() # Automatically uses Wilcoxon (if 2 groups) or Kruskal-Wallis (if >2)

p_violin

# ==========================================
# 7. HEATMAP
# ==========================================
# Ensure we only try to plot signature genes that actually exist in your matrix
available_genes <- intersect(fibroblast_signatures$TSI_Signature, colnames(fibr_gene_expression_modified_genes))

# Extract the stroma AOI filenames to subset the columns
stroma_aois <- stroma_data$dcc_filename

# Create a subset matrix: Rows = Signature Genes, Columns = Stroma AOIs
heatmap_matrix <- as.matrix(fibr_gene_expression_modified_genes[available_genes, stroma_aois])

# Create an annotation dataframe to show the segment_geomx colors on top of the heatmap
annotation_col <- data.frame(
  segment_geomx = stroma_data$segment_geomx
)
rownames(annotation_col) <- stroma_data$dcc_filename

# Generate the Heatmap
pheatmap(
  heatmap_matrix,
  scale = "row",               # Converts raw expression to Z-scores across rows for visualization
  annotation_col = annotation_col,
  show_colnames = FALSE,       # Set to TRUE if you want to see the individual dcc_filenames
  main = "TSI Signature Gene Expression in Stroma"
)

```




Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
